---
title: "GitHub and Gitlab"
subtitle: "Comparing different solutions for accessing Git"
author: "Kieran Martin"
date: "2025-09-04"
categories: [tools, github, gitlab]
image: "thumb.png"
# grid:
#   sidebar-width: 050px
#   body-width: 1200px
#   margin-width: 250px
#   gutter-width: 1.5rem
toc: TRUE
toc-title: "Table of contents"
toc-depth: 5
---

# Introduction

Users of Git will typically want to have a shared repository where they can collaborate with other users. Fortunately, there are excellent solutions for this. In this post I will take a look at two popular tools for this, Gitlab and Github. Other solutions definitely exist, such as

- BitBucket
- OneDev
- Gogs
- Codeberg
- Radicle
- Gitea

And many more.

Note that the aim of this post is try to provide a dispassionate comparison without providing any recommendations; the information in this article is correct to the best of my knowledge and is based on available documentation. Please raise an issue if you spot any inaccuracies.

# What is the purpose of a code repository server

Both Github and Gitlab function primarily as a central repository for Git repositories.
